title: Advanced Micro Devices Q2 Preview: Time to Buy?
text: It's the busiest week of earnings season, with an extensive list of companies scheduled to unveil their quarterly results.

title: What Auto Sales Downturn? This 1 Stock Keeps Posting Soaring Revenue From Automakers
text: NXP Semiconductors reported another big decline in mobile chip sales, but auto sales evened things out. Signs are emerging that the downturn in mobile and other consumer electronics might be coming to an end.

title: Analysts Will Push NXP Semiconductors To A New High
text: NXP Semiconductors NASDAQ: NXP did not have a blowout quarter, but the results are good enough that analysts praise the news. The takeaway from the chatter is that another steady quarter with solid cash flow supported by the company's diversification has come and gone.

title: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) Q2 Earnings Beat, Revenues Fall Y/Y
text: NXP Semiconductors' (NXPI) second-quarter 2023 results reflect strength in the Automotive and Communication Infrastructure & Others markets amid weakness in the Industrial & IoT and Mobile markets.

title: Dow Up 10 Straight Sessions; NXPI Beats, WHR Mixed
text: NXP Semi topped estimates on both top and bottom lines, while Whirlpool beat easily on earnings but missed on revenues.

title: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) Surpasses Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) came out with quarterly earnings of $3.43 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $3.27 per share. This compares to earnings of $4.74 per share a year ago.

title: Compared to Estimates, NXP (NXPI) Q2 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for NXP (NXPI) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: NXP Semiconductors Beats Second-Quarter Goals On Strong Auto Chip Sales
text: NXP Semiconductors beat Wall Street analyst estimates for the second quarter Monday, thanks to continued strong automotive chip sales.

title: NXP forecasts third quarter above estimates on steady automotive demand
text: Chipmaker NXP Semiconductors forecast third-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street targets on Monday, betting that demand from automakers ramping up production will cushion the hit from a weak consumer electronics market.

title: Auto chip maker NXP tops expectations ahead of Texas Instruments earnings
text: NXP Semiconductors N.V. shares rose in the extended session Monday after the chip maker — which gets more than half its sales from auto makers — topped Wall Street expectations.

title: NXP Semiconductors Reports Second Quarter 2023 Results

title: NXP Semiconductors - For Another Day
text: NXP Semiconductors, a Dutch-based semiconductor company, is well-positioned to benefit from developing trends in the auto market, with the auto segment accounting for its largest and fastest-growing revenue stream. Despite its core competence in the semiconductor space, NXP has seen slowing free cash flow dynamics and a declining margin profile, with sub-par working capital dynamics impacting the operating cash flow position. Forward valuations look pricey and we dislike the risk-reward on the charts.

title: NXP Semiconductors Announces Conference Call to Review Second Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: EINDHOVEN, The Netherlands, July 06, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NASDAQ: NXPI) today announced it will release financial results for the second quarter 2023 after the close of normal trading on the NASDAQ Global Select Market on Monday, July 24, 2023. The company will host a conference call with the financial community on Tuesday, July 25, 2023 at 8:00 a.m. U.S. Eastern Daylight Time (EDT).

title: NXPI or MPWR: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors looking for stocks in the Semiconductor - Analog and Mixed sector might want to consider either NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) or Monolithic Power (MPWR). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?
